key_insight: Peak principal stress in the Rubin Observatory M1M3 mirror can be estimated quickly by root-sum-square combining precomputed unit-mode stress fields, mirroring how bending-mode displacements are combined.
engineering_relevance: This provides near-instant stress-margin checks and actuator-force guidance for active optics operations without rerunning full finite element simulations.
method: Run NASTRAN to compute unit displacement and unit stress fields for the first 20 bending modes.
Combine representative multi-mode corrections with an RSS rule to predict peak major principal stress.
Validate RSS stress predictions against full NASTRAN simulations for astigmatism, coma, and spherical-mode combinations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports agreement within a few percent between RSS-based peak principal stress predictions and NASTRAN results for representative multi-mode cases.
watch_item: The approach is validated on representative mode combinations, so broader load cases or nonlinearity near stress limits may still require full FE verification.
paper_title: Determining stress-based bending mode limits for the Vera C. Rubin Observatory M1M3 active mirror system
